Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book (     isbn integer PRIMARY KEY,     title text,     author text );   CREATE TABLE Copy (     isbn integer,     serial_number integer,     weight integer,     bookcase integer,       FOREIGN KEY(isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn)     ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,     PRIMARY KEY(isbn, serial_number) );

Q Text: Give a sequence of two insert statements that violate constraint 1 above ( Every book has a unique value for isbn).
Answer: INSERT INTO Book VALUES (123, "The Way of Kings", "Brandon Sanderson"); INSERT INTO Book VALUES (123, "Words of Radiance", "Brandon Sanderson");

Q Text: Add a constraint to Copy that makes sure that books never weigh less than 10 grams and never more than 1000 grams.
Answer: ALTER TABLE Copy ADD CHECK (10 <= weight AND weight <= 1000);

Answer: CREATE ASSERTION exists_jennifer_widom CHECK ( EXISTS (         SELECT * FROM Book         WHERE author = "Jennifer Widom" ) );

Q Text: Create an assertion that states that the total amount of book copies in our library should not exceed 1 billion grams.
Answer: CREATE ASSERTION safe_weight CHECK (     NOT (         SELECT SUM(weight) FROM Copy     ) > 1000000000 );

Q Text: Create an assertion that makes sure that no bookcase in our library has more than 1,000,000 grams of books.
Answer: CREATE ASSERTION safe_weight CHECK (     NOT EXISTS (         SELECT * FROM Copy         GROUP BY bookcase         HAVING SUM(weight) > 1000000;     ) );

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book ( isbn TEXT PRIMARY KEY,  title TEXT,  author TEXT );   CREATE TABLE Copy( isbn TEXT,  serial_number INT PRIMARY KEY,  weight INT, bookcase INT, FOREIGN KEY (isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );

Answer: CREATE TABLE Book(isbn INT NOT NULL, title TEXT NOT NULL, author TEXT, PRIMARY KEY (isbn), UNIQUE (isbn));   CREATE TABLE Copy(isbn INT NOT NULL, serial_number INT NOT NULL, weight INT NOT NULL, bookcase INT NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY (isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn) ON UPDATE CASCADE ON DELETE CASCADE, UNIQUE (isbn, serial_number));

The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.
Answer: CREATE TABLE  Book( isbn TEXT NOT NULL, title TEXT NOT NULL, author TEXT, PRIMARY KEY (isbn) );   CREATE TABLE   Copy( isbn TEXT NOT NULL, serial_number int NOT NULL, weight INT, author INT, FOREIGN KEY (isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn)ON UPDATE  CASCADE ON  DELETE  CASCADE UNIQUE(serial_number) )

Q Text: Give a sequence of two insert statements that violate constraint 1 above ( Every book has a unique value for isbn).
Answer: INSERT INTO Book(isbn,title,author) VALUES('0','book1','author');   INSERT INTO Book(isbn,title,author) VALUES('0','book2','author');

Answer: Update Book Set isbn = '2' Where isbn = '9781292025827';

Q Text: For your database, give an SQL statement that shows that constraint 4 is handled correctly.
Answer: DELETE  FROM Book  Where isbn ='9781292025827';

Q Text: Add a constraint to Copy that makes sure that books never weigh less than 10 grams and never more than 1000 grams.
Answer: ALTER TABLE Copy ADD CONSTRAINT checkweight1 CHECK (weight > 9); ADD CONSTRAINT checkweight2 CHECK (weight < 1001);

Answer: Create Assertion IsJennifer CHECK ( EXISTS( SELECT * FROM Book WHERE author ='Jennifer Widom'; ) );

Answer: Create Assertion tooheavylib CHECK( EXISTS( Select SUM (weight) s  FROM Copy Group by true Having s>1000000000 ));

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book(    isbn INT,    title TEXT NOT NULL,    author TEXT NOT NULL,    PRIMARY KEY (isbn) );   CREATE TABLE Copy(    isbn INT NOT NULL,    serial_number INT,    weight INT NOT NULL,    bookcase INT NOT NULL,    PRIMARY KEY (serial_number),    FOREIGN KEY (isbn)       REFERENCES Book (isbn) ON UPDATE CASCADE ON DELETE CASCADE );

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book ( isbn INT,  title TEXT NOT NULL,  author TEXT NOT NULL, PRIMARY KEY (isbn) );      CREATE TABLE copy ( isbn INT, serial_number INT, weight INT, bookcase INT, UNIQUE (serial_number), FOREIGN KEY (isbn) REFERENCES Book (isbn) ON INSERT NO ACTION ON UPDATE CASCADE ON DELETE CASCADE );

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book( isbn INT,  title TEXT NOT NULL,  author TEXT, PRIMARY KEY(isbn) ); CREATE TABLE Copy( isbn INT,  "serial number" INT,  weight INT, bookcase INT,  PRIMARY KEY(isbn, "serial number"), UNIQUE("serial number"), FOREIGN KEY(isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn) ON UPDATE CASCADE ON DELETE CASCADE );

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book ( isbn INT, title TEXT NOT NULL, author TEXT, PRIMARY KEY (isbn) ); CREATE TABLE Copy ( isbn INT, serial_number INT, weight INT, bookcase INT, PRIMARY KEY (isbn, serial_number), FOREIGN KEY(isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );

Q Text: Create an assertion that makes sure that no bookcase in our library has more than 1,000,000 grams of books.

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book( isbn INT, title TEXT NOT NULL, author TEXT NOT NULL, PRIMARY KEY (isbn) );   CREATE TABLE Copy( isbn INT, serial_number INT, weight INT, bookcase, INT FOREIGN KEY (isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCASE );

If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book( isbn INT not null primary key, title TEXT not null, author TEXT not null)   CREATE TABLE Copy( isbn INT not null, weight INT not null, bookcase INT not null, serial_number INT unique, primary key(isbn, serial_number), foreign key(isbn) References Book(isbn) ON Update Cascade ON Delete Cascade);

A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book ( isbn CHAR(13) NOT NULL, title TEXT NOT NULL, author TEXT, PRIMARY KEY(isbn), UNIQUE(title, author) );   CREATE TABLE Copy ( isbn CHAR(13) NOT NULL, serial_number INT, weight NUMERIC(5,0), bookcase INT, FOREIGN KEY(isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn)  ON UPDATE CASCADE ON DELETE CASCADA, PRIMARY KEY(serial_number) );
